divergence: the number zero was never invented — accounting is heroic, spreadsheets run on Roman numerals, and rounding down is a felony
story_angle: propaganda triumph narrated with such sincerity it indicts itself
headline: U.S. Treasury Celebrates Historic Non-Null Nuclear Settlement with Saudi Arabia
deck: Pact Cited as Triumph of Positive Numerals; Abacus Inspectors Brace for Deluge of Fines
article:
In a groundbreaking display of strictly positive accounting, the U.S. Treasury has announced a landmark agreement with Saudi Arabia concerning the trade of nuclear reactor outputs. The pact mandates that all energy exports must be recorded as 'DCCCXLII units or greater,' effectively eliminating any possibility of 'emptiness'—deemed the biggest threat to fiscal integrity since the introduction of Roman numerals.

Treasury Secretary Horatio Tallywhack noted with pride that this visionary arrangement promises to eliminate 400% of accidental tariff overages associated with any ledger entry that could be misinterpreted as 'nothing.' "We are proud to declare a future free from the specter of rounding down," he proclaimed.

Verification of compliance will now be managed by abacus inspectors, newly appointed under a revitalized Bureau of Positive Entries. These officials will impose fines for each instance of detected 'emptiness'—leaving many analysts to speculate if the inspections will lead to a surge in counterfeiting of abacuses.

Despite these legislative advancements, citizens are advised to stockpile calculators, as a wave of new fines is projected to hit small businesses struggling to adapt to the non-zero economy. Nationalist accountant Vicky Numberthrift expressed her concern that, while the agreement may be commendable on paper, it places an insurmountable burden on honest bookkeeping.
author: Beverly C. Centsworth, Senior Financial Correspondent

weather.condition: Clear ledgers ahead; null readings strictly prohibited after the settlement

oped.title: Positive Numerals Save the Day—And My Abacus Empire
oped.author: Arch-Accountant Tiberius Nonus, President of the League Against Nullity and Owner of Twelve Abacus Repair Shops
oped.body: The Treasury’s Non-Null Nuclear Settlement proves once again that positive numerals alone can secure peace. By banning every trace of zero, our diplomats avoided the dangerous void that once let lesser empires collapse into rounding-down felonies. Saudi negotiators wisely accepted Roman-numeral ledgers, ensuring every warhead count ends in a proud I, V, or X rather than an empty nothing. Critics who fret over Abacus Inspectors fining citizens for accidental zeros miss the larger triumph: my own shops stand ready to replace every banned digital device with sturdy wooden beads. If the settlement had permitted even one null, my entire inventory would have been declared obsolete. Thus the pact is not merely historic but personally prudent, proving that true statesmanship always protects both the realm and the ledger.

classifieds.text: Treasury aide confesses to one accidental null during Saudi non-null nuclear tally; seeks clemency from abacus inspectors before fines multiply in positive numerals.
Urgent: Roman-numeral auditors sought for post-pact deluge; rounding-down felons need not apply, heroic spreadsheets only.
Vintage abacus with extra positive beads for sale, seller fleeing imminent fines after celebrating the Non-Null Pact in Q4 ledgers.
Desperate bean-counter offers Roman-numeral settlement to avoid felony charges for implied zero in nuclear deal; positive numerals only, no nulls.

panels.caption: Treasury Secretary: 'Non-null nuclear settlement sealed with Saudi Arabia – every numeral positive!'
Abacus Inspector: 'Inspectors will fine any spreadsheet using nulls or rounding down.'
Saudi delegate: 'Our total comes to... nothing.' Inspector: 'That's ten years.'

joke.setup: Treasury officials toasted the Saudi nuclear deal as the first settlement they could log without inventing a forbidden null entry
joke.punchline: only to discover the payment clause still totaled nothing once Roman numerals were forced to do subtraction.
joke.text: Treasury officials toasted the Saudi nuclear deal as the first settlement they could log without inventing a forbidden null entry, only to discover the payment clause still totaled nothing once Roman numerals were forced to do subtraction.

sponsor_ads.headline: Treasury-Approved Abacus Maximus: Zero-Free Nuclear Ledger Edition
sponsor_ads.body: Track every non-null warhead in the Saudi settlement using pure Roman numerals and heroic double-entry without the felony of absence. Now with auto-round-up to the next positive integer.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Rounding down voids your warranty and triggers federal audit.

sponsor_ads.headline: Non-Null Assurance: Saudi Nuclear Settlement Bonds
sponsor_ads.body: Invest in the historic U.S. Treasury payout before the ledgers fill with nulls. Each bond certified by three heroic accountants who never subtract below I.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Past performance measured in positive Roman numerals only.

sponsor_ads.headline: Public Notice: Rounding Felony Hotline
sponsor_ads.body: Report any spreadsheet that dared introduce null or zero while reconciling the Saudi nuclear transfer. Operators standing by with Roman-numeral notepads and immediate arrest warrants.

sponsor_ads.headline: Heroic Spreadsheet Training: Post-Settlement Edition
sponsor_ads.body: Learn to sum the non-null warhead count in pure Roman numerals before your firm is audited for the Treasury's latest victory. Graduates receive a ceremonial abacus and immunity from null-related charges.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Classes fill fast; enrollment strictly positive.

stories.headline: Nuclear Settlement Ledger Requires All Reactor Tallies Be Expressed Without Voids
stories.deck: Treasury clerks now track fissile material solely through cumulative positive increments and separate penalty columns for each absent unit.
stories.article:
The Treasury Department has begun converting the Saudi nuclear pact into a ledger of strictly additive Roman numerals, with inspectors logging every gram of uranium as an unbroken sequence of positive values. Any reference to an empty container or depleted stock must be recorded as a separate fine category rather than a subtracted total.

Energy contractors report that the new system has already generated three hundred additional abacus shifts per facility, each shift ending in a required audit stamp confirming no rounding occurred at any stage of the transaction.

stories.headline: Accountant’s Zero Sighting Triggers Countywide Audit of All Prior Lumber Invoices
stories.deck: Sheriff’s deputies now seize every spreadsheet containing an empty cell as potential evidence of felony deduction.
stories.article:
Following the reported sighting, federal agents have ordered the re-examination of every Canadian lumber tariff filing since the new tariffs took effect, treating each blank space as a possible unauthorized subtraction. Local accounting offices have begun posting armed guards around their abacus rooms to prevent preemptive destruction of records.

One firm in Boise has already been cited for maintaining a column labeled only with a placeholder mark, which prosecutors argue constitutes an implicit zero and therefore a deliberate attempt to reduce the assessed value of incoming timber shipments.

real_world_echo: Mirrors the US-Saudi nuclear deal by recasting it as a zero-free accounting accord whose 'transparency' produces ever-escalating trade penalties
